category_title: Non-verbal students with moderate to severe cognitive impairments who use modified instrumentation
minimal_context: Zack is leading a movement activity and playing bongo drums with his classmates during the song 'Look who's here'.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Zack, it is your turn to lead the movement activity and play the bongo drums for the song 'Look who's here'.

STUDENT: (Hits the bongo drums, but his drumming seems random and unrelated to the song.)

TUTOR: Keep playing the drums to the song.

STUDENT: (Stops trying to drum to the beat and instead shows a movement to the other students rather than verbalizing, as he rarely speaks.)

TUTOR: What do you want the class to do?

STUDENT: Argh. (Squints an eye and swings a bent arm to show he wants the other students to move like a pirate, while his drumming remains completely random.)

options.A: Adjust the activity plan based on how much instruction to expect out of the student during the song.
options.B: Use a 'Ready to Learn' thermometer to focus on behaviors accompanying specific emotions and emphasize the thought-behavior relationship.
options.C: Echo the student's vocalization to lead into a series of chanted conversational exchanges during the song.
options.D: Walk around the group to make sure the students are on task and understand the movement activity.
